title: chaerophyllum plicatum
intro: Chaerophyllum plicatum, also known as curled chervil or curled parsley, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Chaerophyllum plicatum can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
